name: 4. 고등 가주어·가목적어 200
description: 가주어 It ~ to V/that절, 가목적어 It(5형식), 의미상 주어 for/of + 목적격, There is/are 구문, It seems/appears that 200개 (고등 수준)

words.w: It is important to study hard.
words.m: [가주어 It ~ to V] 열심히 공부하는 것이 중요하다. (It = 가주어, to V = 진주어)

words.w: It is fun to play games.
words.m: [가주어 It ~ to V] 게임하는 것이 재미있다.

words.w: It is difficult to learn Chinese.

words.w: It is true that he is honest.

words.w: It is heartbreaking that they split.

words.m: [가주어 It ~ that 절] 꿀이 달다는 것은 알려져 있다.

words.w: I found it touching to see them.

words.w: There is nothing to worry about.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 걱정할 것이 없다.

words.w: There are some problems to solve.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 풀어야 할 문제가 있다.

words.w: There must be a reason.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 이유가 있을 것이다.

words.w: There seems to be no way out.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 나갈 방법이 없는 것 같다. (There seems to be)

words.w: There used to be a park here.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 여기 공원이 있었다. (There used to be)

words.w: There is a cat sleeping.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 고양이가 자고 있다.

words.w: There are children playing outside.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 아이들이 밖에서 놀고 있다.

words.w: There was a loud noise.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 큰 소리가 있었다.

words.w: There were three options.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 세 가지 선택권이 있었다.

words.w: There is a good chance he'll win.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 그가 이길 좋은 기회가 있다.

words.w: There are rumors about them.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 그들에 대한 소문이 있다.

words.w: There has been a mistake.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 실수가 있었다. (There has been)

words.w: There will be rain tomorrow.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 내일 비가 올 것이다.

words.w: There is no place like home.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 집만 한 곳이 없다.

words.w: There must have been a fire.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 화재가 있었을 것이다. (과거 추측)

words.w: There appears to be a problem.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 문제가 있는 것 같다. (There appears to be)

words.w: There can be no doubt.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 의심의 여지가 없다.

words.w: There used to be a bookstore here.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 여기 서점이 있었다.

words.w: There is something missing.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 빠진 것이 있다.

words.w: There are no survivors.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 생존자가 없다.

words.w: There was a time when I believed.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 내가 믿었던 시절이 있었다.

words.w: There is little chance of rain.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 비 올 가능성이 거의 없다.

words.w: There are two sides to every story.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 모든 이야기에는 두 면이 있다.

words.w: There is something special about her.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 그녀에게는 특별한 무엇이 있다.

words.w: There have been attempts to reach him.
words.m: [There is/are 구문] 그에게 연락하려는 시도가 있었다.

words.w: It seems that he is tired.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 그는 피곤한 것 같다.

words.w: It appears that she is angry.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 그녀는 화난 것 같다.

words.w: It seems that they lost.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 그들이 진 것 같다.

words.w: It appears to be a mistake.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 실수인 것 같다. (It appears to be)

words.w: It seems like a good idea.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 좋은 생각인 것 같다. (It seems like)

words.w: It seems to me that he is right.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 내 생각엔 그가 맞는 것 같다.

words.w: It appears to me that she lied.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 내가 보기엔 그녀가 거짓말한 것 같다.

words.w: It seems certain that they will win.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 그들이 이길 것이 확실해 보인다.

words.w: It appears obvious that he's hiding something.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 그가 뭔가 숨기는 것이 분명해 보인다.

words.w: It seems unlikely that he'll come.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 그가 올 것 같지 않다.

words.w: It appears that the weather will improve.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 날씨가 좋아질 것 같다.

words.w: It seems strange that no one came.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 아무도 오지 않은 것이 이상해 보인다.

words.w: It seems that we agree.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 우리가 동의하는 것 같다.

words.w: It appears that he forgot.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 그가 잊은 것 같다.

words.w: It seems that she has gone home.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 그녀가 집에 간 것 같다.

words.w: It seems as if time stopped.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 시간이 멈춘 것 같다. (It seems as if)

words.w: It appears that they are twins.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 그들은 쌍둥이인 것 같다.

words.w: It seems that everyone knows.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 모두가 아는 것 같다.

words.w: It appears to work fine.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 제대로 작동하는 것 같다.

words.w: It seems unusual that he's late.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 그가 늦는 것이 드물어 보인다.

words.w: It seems that she missed the bus.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 그녀가 버스를 놓친 것 같다.

words.w: It appears that the plan worked.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 계획이 성공한 것 같다.

words.w: It seemed that no one cared.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 아무도 신경 쓰지 않는 것 같았다.

words.w: It seems clear that he is sick.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 그가 아픈 것이 분명해 보인다.

words.w: It appears that we are lost.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 우리가 길을 잃은 것 같다.

words.w: It seems that the war ended.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 전쟁이 끝난 것 같다.

words.w: It appears she changed her mind.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 그녀가 마음을 바꾼 것 같다.

words.w: It seems impossible at first.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 처음에는 불가능해 보인다.

words.w: It appears that she's been waiting.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 그녀가 기다리고 있던 것 같다.

words.w: It seems that the rumor is true.
words.m: [It seems/appears that] 소문이 사실인 것 같다.
